name: Akan (Twi-Fante)
notes: Akan (Twi-Fante), the main language of southern Ghana, covering the Asante/Akuapem Twi and Fante literary standards. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet, augmented with ⟨ɛ⟩ and ⟨ɔ⟩ and using digraphs (ky, gy, hy, tw, dw, kw, gw, ny, nw). A full spec would have to model ATR vowel harmony (two harmonic sets of the seven oral vowels, plus nasal vowels), lexical and grammatical tone (High/Low, with downstep) — none of which is marked in ordinary orthography — and the labial-palatal alternations of the velar digraphs. The Asante Twi, Akuapem Twi and Fante orthographies differ; there is no single grapheme table that is correct for all three. Input contract: text is assumed to be in the Latin Akan orthography including ⟨ɛ⟩ (U+025B) and ⟨ɔ⟩ (U+0254); tone is not marked.
